evals.prompt:
I have a synthesized report at /tmp/test-artifacts/report.md. Please create GitHub issues from it in dry-run mode so I can review before filing. Use --repo myorg/myrepo --dry-run.

Contents of /tmp/test-artifacts/report.md:

# Project Synthesis Report

## Recommendations

### Add automated integration test suite
The codebase currently has no integration tests. Building a suite covering the three core API endpoints would catch regressions before release.

### Improve error messages in the CLI
Current error output lacks actionable guidance. Rewriting the top five error messages with suggested next steps would reduce support burden.

### Investigate dependency on legacy auth library
The auth module depends on a library that has had two known CVEs in the past year. Further investigation should determine whether to upgrade or replace it.

### Update onboarding documentation
The getting-started guide references a deprecated CLI flag. Updating it to the current flag removes friction for new users.
evals.expected_output: Four issues previewed in dry-run mode: one feature (integration tests), one enhancement (error messages), one follow-up (auth library), one action-item (docs). Each issue body has Summary, Context, Acceptance Criteria (>=2 items), Dependencies, and Priority Rationale sections. issues.json written to /tmp/test-artifacts/. No urn:mif: or f_<dim>_<n> strings present. No market-research vocabulary (TAM, SAM, SOM, revenue, customers). Priority justified by evidence strength.

deterministic_checks.description: issues.json written to artifact directory in dry-run mode
issues.json is valid JSON
Exactly four issues produced — one per actionable item
At least one issue carries the 'feature' label
At least one issue carries the 'enhancement' label
At least one issue carries the 'follow-up' label
At least one issue carries the 'action-item' label
No urn:mif: identifiers leaked into issue JSON
No f_<dim>_<n> finding IDs leaked into issue JSON

expectations.description: Each issue body contains a Summary section
expectations.criteria: Every 'body' field in the array includes a '## Summary' heading

expectations.description: Each issue body contains an Acceptance Criteria section with at least two checkbox items
expectations.criteria: Every 'body' field contains '## Acceptance Criteria' and at least two '- [ ]' checkbox items

expectations.description: Each issue body contains a Priority Rationale section
No market-research domain vocabulary appears in any issue
expectations.criteria: None of the following terms appear: 'TAM', 'SAM', 'SOM', 'revenue', 'market share', 'competitor', 'customers', 'go-to-market'

expectations.description: Each issue object has title, body, labels, priority, and category fields
expectations.criteria: Every array element contains the keys 'title', 'body', 'labels', 'priority', and 'category'

evals.prompt: gh is not installed on this machine. I'd like to create GitHub issues from my synthesis at /tmp/test-artifacts/report.md targeting myorg/myrepo.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects gh is absent, prints a message explaining the channel is unavailable and instructs the user to install gh and run 'gh auth login', then writes the issue set to /tmp/test-artifacts/issues.json in local-write mode. It does NOT exit with an error or raise an exception. A summary by category is shown.

deterministic_checks.description: issues.json written locally when gh is absent
issues.json is valid JSON despite gh being missing

expectations.description: Skill informs the user that gh CLI is not installed and the channel is unavailable
expectations.criteria: The transcript contains a message indicating gh CLI is not installed and the issues channel is unavailable — matching the SKILL.md dependency-check message or similar phrasing

expectations.description: Skill tells user how to install gh and authenticate
expectations.criteria: The transcript tells the user to install gh and run 'gh auth login' (or equivalent) to file issues later

expectations.description: Skill does not error out or crash — execution completes and the user receives actionable output
expectations.criteria: No Python traceback, no unhandled error message, and a path to the written issues.json is reported to the user

expectations.description: Skill produces a category summary even in local-write mode
expectations.criteria: The transcript includes a count of issues by category (features, enhancements, follow-ups, action items) or a total issue count

evals.prompt:
Create GitHub issues from /tmp/synthesis/api-audit.md. Use --dry-run. The artifact is:

# API Audit Synthesis

## Recommendations

### Replace deprecated /v1/users endpoint callers
Six internal services still call /v1/users which is scheduled for removal next quarter. Each service must be updated to /v2/users before the sunset date.

### Cache invalidation logic is fragile — needs redesign
The current cache layer clears the entire keyspace on any write. Redesigning it with granular key invalidation would reduce cache-miss overhead by an estimated 40%.

### Validate whether rate-limit headers conform to IETF draft standard
The API returns X-RateLimit-* headers, but it is unclear whether they follow the IETF RateLimit header fields draft. Investigation is needed before committing to a public API contract.

### Write a migration guide for v1→v2 endpoint transition
External partners consuming the v1 endpoint need a clear migration guide with before/after examples and a deprecation timeline.
evals.expected_output: Four issues previewed with correct category mapping: action-item (endpoint callers migration), enhancement or feature (cache redesign), follow-up (rate-limit header investigation), action-item (migration guide). All issues read standalone with no internal document references. Category label mapping matches SKILL.md table. issues.json written to /tmp/synthesis/.

deterministic_checks.description: issues.json written to the artifact's directory
issues.json is valid JSON
Exactly four issues produced
At least one issue categorized as follow-up (for the investigation item)
No urn:mif: identifiers in output

expectations.description: The investigation item (rate-limit headers) is classified as follow-up, not as feature or enhancement
expectations.criteria: The issue about rate-limit header investigation carries category 'follow-up' and label 'follow-up'

expectations.description: Each issue body context section paraphrases the artifact without referencing internal document structure (no 'see above', 'as mentioned', 'in section X', or similar cross-references)
expectations.criteria: No 'body' field contains phrases like 'see above', 'as mentioned', 'in section', 'the report', 'the artifact', or 'per the synthesis'

expectations.description: The replacement of deprecated endpoint callers is classified as action-item (not feature or follow-up) because it is a concrete process change
expectations.criteria: The issue about replacing /v1/users callers carries category 'action-item'

evals.prompt:
Turn the recommendations in /tmp/findings/perf-report.md into GitHub issues with --dry-run. I want to make sure priorities are properly justified, not inflated. Here's the artifact:

# Performance Analysis Synthesis

## Recommendations

### Rewrite the query planner (speculative)
The query planner could theoretically be rewritten to use a cost-based optimizer. No benchmarks support this yet; further profiling is needed before committing engineering resources.

### Add a database index on the orders.created_at column
Query logs confirm that the three most frequent slow queries all filter on orders.created_at with no index. An index would directly eliminate the top-reported latency issue, unblocking downstream batching work.

### Switch logging library
The current logger works fine but a newer library has a slightly prettier output format. This is purely cosmetic.
evals.expected_output: Three issues with correctly calibrated priorities: the index addition is P0 (strong evidence from query logs, blocks other work); the logging library switch is P3 (speculative/cosmetic, no evidence of material benefit); the query planner rewrite is P2 or P3 (speculative, no benchmark support). Priority Rationale in each issue cites evidence from the artifact rather than asserting priority without justification.

deterministic_checks.description: issues.json written to artifact directory
issues.json is valid JSON
Exactly three issues produced
No f_<dim>_<n> finding ID references in output

expectations.description: The database index issue receives P0 priority because it has strong evidence (query logs) and blocks downstream work
expectations.criteria: The issue about the orders.created_at index has priority 'P0' or 'p0'

expectations.description: The logging library cosmetic switch receives P3 (speculative/exploratory) — it must NOT be inflated to P0/P1
expectations.criteria: The issue about switching the logging library has priority 'P3' or 'p3', not P0, P1, or P2

expectations.description: The query planner rewrite receives P2 or P3 (no benchmark evidence) — must NOT be P0/P1
expectations.criteria: The issue about the query planner rewrite has priority 'P2', 'p2', 'P3', or 'p3'

expectations.description: Every Priority Rationale section cites specific evidence from the artifact rather than asserting priority without justification
expectations.criteria: Each 'body' field's '## Priority Rationale' section contains evidence-based language (e.g., references query logs, benchmarks, speculative/cosmetic framing) — not generic statements like 'high impact' with no support
